analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes ISC BIND DNS server logs, focusing on timestamp normalization from BIND's native format to standardized epoch seconds. It specifically handles network-level DNS server events from the Internet Systems Consortium's BIND implementation.
optimization_opportunities: Include error handling for malformed timestamps
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: isc_bind-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Network Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:21:28.703778

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format

-- Constants
local OCSF_CLASS_UID = 1001
local OCSF_CATEGORY_UID = 1
local TIMESTAMP_MULTIPLIER = 1000

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize minimal OCSF-compliant output structure
    local output = {
        class_uid = OCSF_CLASS_UID,
        class_name = "Network Activity", 
        category_uid = OCSF_CATEGORY_UID,
        category_name = "Network Activity",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "BIND DNS",
                vendor_name = "ISC"
            },
            version = "1.0.0"
        }
    }

    -- Extract and validate timestamp
    local timestamp
    if record.unmapped and record.unmapped.timestamp then
        timestamp = tonumber(record.unmapped.timestamp)
        if timestamp then
            -- Convert to milliseconds if needed
            output.time = timestamp < 1000000000000 and timestamp * TIMESTAMP_MULTIPLIER or timestamp
        end
    end

    -- Set default timestamp if none found
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time() * TIMESTAMP_MULTIPLIER
    end

    -- DNS-specific field mappings
    if record.unmapped then
        -- Add additional DNS fields with validation
        if record.unmapped.query_name then
            output.dns = output.dns or {}
            output.dns.query_name = record.unmapped.query_name
        end
        
        if record.unmapped.query_type then
            output.dns = output.dns or {}
            output.dns.query_type = record.unmapped.query_type
        end
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid OCSF class_uid"
    end

    -- Add observability metadata
    output.metadata = output.metadata or {}
    output.metadata.parser_version = "1.0.0"
    output.metadata.processing_time = os_time()

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

--[[ Test Cases
local test1 = safe_transform({
    unmapped = {
        timestamp = "1634567890",
        query_name = "example.com",
        query_type = "A"
    }
})

local test2 = safe_transform(nil)

local test3 = safe_transform({
    unmapped = {
        timestamp = "invalid"
    }
})
--]]
monitoring_recommendations: Alert on repeated field mapping failures
Track DNS-specific field extraction success rate
